Specifies unique 2-bit IDs for processors to differentiate system logs and core dumps.

# Purpose
This C header file, `chip_id.h`, defines unique identifiers for processors in multi-processor devices. These identifiers, `CORE_ID_MAIN_MCU` and `CORE_ID_BLE`, are used to differentiate the source of system logs and core dumps. Each identifier must be unique within a platform and fit within 2 bits, which allows for a maximum of four distinct processor IDs. If a device includes more than four processors that generate logs or core dumps, the current system will need modification to accommodate additional identifiers.
